details: The “Financial Assistance for Funeral Expenses” scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu and Kashmir, is a welfare program for registered workers. Under this scheme, ₹5,000/- is provided as funeral assistance to the nominee or legal heir upon the death of the registered worker.
eligibility: The applicant must be the nominee or legal heir of the deceased worker.
The deceased worker must have been registered with the Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
The deceased worker must have been an active member of the Board at the time of death.
The scheme can be applied for only once.

offline: of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
enter your details, and sign in to your account.
After logging in, click on "Scheme Registration" and select "Apply for Scheme."
Choose the scheme you wish to apply for in order to avail the benefits.
Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
Applicant can track their application status online through the
of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

documents_required: Identity Card of the Deceased Worker
Identity Proof of the Nominee/Legal Heir
Certificate of Being a Nominee/legal Heir

faqs.question: What is the Financial Assistance for Funeral Expenses Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial support to cover the funeral expenses of a deceased registered worker. The assistance is given to the worker's nominee or legal heirs.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The nominee or legal heir of a registered worker with the Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board is eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What is the amount of financial assistance provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: An amount of ₹5,000/- is provided as funeral assistance to the nominee or legal heirs of the deceased worker.

faqs.question: Is there any condition for the worker's registration status?
faqs.answer: Yes, the deceased worker must have been a registered and active member of the Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board at the time of death.

faqs.question: Can the scheme be applied for more than once?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme can be applied for only once, and the assistance is provided for each registered worker only once after their death.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: You will typically need to provide the death certificate of the worker, the nominee’s identity proof, and any other documents as required by the Welfare Board.

faqs.question: How do I apply for the funeral assistance scheme?
faqs.answer: The nominee or legal heir can apply online by logging into the Welfare Board's official website or visiting the nearest Labour Department office and submitting the necessary documents.

faqs.question: Can multiple legal heirs apply for the funeral assistance?
faqs.answer: No, only one nominee or legal heir can apply for the funeral assistance under this scheme.

faqs.question: How can I track the status of my application?
faqs.answer: You can track the status of your application by logging into the Welfare Board’s official website or by contacting the relevant department at the Labour Office where you submitted your application.

faqs.question: Can I apply for the scheme if the worker was registered under another state?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is specifically for registered workers under the Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Only those registered in Jammu and Kashmir are eligible.
